food_name: Longan, fresh, shelled, edible flesh (Dimocarpus longan)
region_of_origin: South and Southeast Asia; commercially cultivated in Thailand, China, Vietnam, and Malaysia
food_subgroup: Fresh tropical fruit — edible translucent white flesh of shelled longan, seed removed

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has limited specific coverage of fresh shelled longan flesh. FOODON_03301710 is the closest available parent node for longan as a tropical drupe fruit. No exact term for shelled edible flesh exists in current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_description: Longan — FoodEx2 category under tropical and subtropical fruits (fresh)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC represents longan as a fruit category. No sub-code specifically for shelled edible flesh; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for longan fruit in current release. Not fabricated.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of longan is approximately 10 fruits, yielding around 80–100g of edible flesh after shelling and seed removal.

nutrient_source.title: Longan, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 09159) cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore / FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for longan raw (FDC ID 167762), corroborated by HPB Singapore Foodo and FAO/INFOODS SEA regional table. Values are consistent across sources. Vitamin C value of 84mg/100g is consistent with USDA (84mg) and FAO/INFOODS SEA data for fresh longan flesh.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Longan — University of Sydney GI Database and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference values
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~40 for fresh longan is referenced from University of Sydney GI Database entries for longan and corroborated by FAO/INFOODS SEA regional data. GL calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 15) / 100 = 6.0 per 100g serving. Confidence 0.9 — direct longan GI entry exists in the University of Sydney database.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (1mg/100g) with moderate potassium (266mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Longan is suitable for hypertensive patients — its negligible sodium content (1mg/100g) and moderate potassium (266mg/100g) support favourable blood pressure regulation without dietary restriction.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~40) but moderate carbohydrate density (15g/100g) with high sugar (14g/100g); portion control is critical
type2_diabetes.guidance: A single 100g serving (approximately 10 fruits) carries a low glycaemic load (~6) and is manageable for T2DM patients; however, the social eating pattern of consuming 20–30 longan per sitting (200–300g flesh, 30–45g carbohydrate) substantially elevates glycaemic impact and must be counselled against.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0g/100g), zero cholesterol, high Vitamin C (84mg/100g) with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Longan poses no cardiovascular risk from fat or cholesterol and its high Vitamin C content (84mg/100g) provides antioxidant support relevant to endothelial health; moderate portion consumption is appropriate for CVD patients.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High Vitamin C (84mg/100g) supports collagen synthesis and anti-inflammatory antioxidant activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Longan's high Vitamin C content (84mg/100g) supports collagen synthesis and provides anti-inflammatory antioxidant properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Longan (龙眼 / 龍眼, literally 'dragon eye') is a small, round tropical fruit in the soapberry family (Sapindaceae), closely related to lychee and rambutan. The fruit has a thin, brittle brown shell that is cracked by hand to reveal translucent, whitish-cream flesh surrounding a single dark brown seed — the visual resemblance of the flesh-encased seed to an eyeball gives the fruit its evocative Chinese name. Longan has a sweeter, less acidic flavour than lychee, with a distinctive musky, floral fragrance. It is widely consumed fresh in Singapore Chinese households during the longan season, and is also sold dried (桂圆 / gui yuan) year-round for use in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) sweet soups, longan red date tea, and herbal tonics. Nutritionally, fresh longan flesh is notable for its exceptional Vitamin C content (84mg/100g — comparable to orange), low GI (~40), very low sodium (1mg/100g), and moderate potassium (266mg/100g). The primary clinical concern for T2DM patients is not the per-fruit carbohydrate load but the social eating pattern: 10 fruits (100g flesh, GL ~6) is manageable, but consuming 30–50 fruits per sitting — common during Chinese New Year gifting and family gatherings — delivers 45–75g carbohydrate and transforms a low-GL snack into a significant glycaemic event. Dried longan (桂圆) has approximately 4× the sugar concentration of fresh longan and should be treated as a high-sugar dried fruit. Longan is commercially cultivated primarily in Thailand (largest global exporter), China, Vietnam, and Malaysia, and is available fresh at Singapore wet markets and supermarkets.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values for fresh longan flesh are well-documented in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 167762), corroborated by HPB Singapore Foodo and FAO/INFOODS SEA regional food composition tables. GI value (~40) is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from approved primary databases with consistent cross-source agreement.
notes: Saturated fat is confirmed zero (0.0g/100g) per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — longan flesh contains negligible saturated fatty acids. Cholesterol is confirmed zero — longan is a plant food. Vitamin A, Vitamin D, Vitamin E, folate, and Vitamin B12 are reported as 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy for fresh longan flesh; these represent confirmed negligible/trace values at or below detection thresholds in the cited source. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for fresh longan flesh is not reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or FAO/INFOODS SEA table as of 2026. water_g set to -1.0: moisture content not separately reported in the primary source used for this record. Dried longan (桂圆/gui yuan) is a distinct product with approximately 4× the sugar concentration of fresh longan and is not covered by this record. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 is applied as closest_parent; no exact FoodOn term exists for shelled fresh longan flesh. FoodEx2 code A00LC applied as closest_parent for longan fruit category. SNOMED CT code not available — not fabricated.
